heading: Application of other laws
label: 4145 Application of other laws
text: To the extent they are not inconsistent with the provisions of this subchapter, the following provisions of subchapter I and title I of the International Claims Settlement Act of 1949, as amended [ et seq.], shall apply to this subchapter: The first sentence of subsection (b) of , all of subsection (c) of and , and subsections (c), (d), (e), and (f) of section 7 of the International Claims Settlement Act of 1949, as amended [].22 U.S.C. 1621section 4101 of this titlesection 4101 of this titlesection 4109 of this title22 U.S.C. 1626
notes: References in TextSubchapter I, referred to in text, was in the original “title I of this Act”, meaning title I of , , which is classified principally to subchapter I (§ 4101 et seq.) of this chapter. For complete classification of title I to the Code, see Tables.act July 3, 1948, ch. 82662 Stat. 1240The International Claims Settlement Act of 1949, referred to in text, is , . Title I of the Act is classified generally to subchapter I (§ 1621 et seq.) of chapter 21 of Title 22, Foreign Relations and Intercourse. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.act Mar. 10, 1950, ch. 5464 Stat. 12section 1621 of Title 22
CodificationSection was formerly classified to section 2017n of the former Appendix to this title prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.
